sections.heading: Cluster Configuration Options
sections.body:
Each Atlas cluster tier comes with a default set of resources.
Atlas provides the following resource configuration options.

sections.heading: Custom Storage Size
sections.body:
Storage size reflects the size of the server root volume. Atlas
clusters deployed onto Google Cloud use SSD (Solid State Disk) persistent storage [^google-disk-docs].

> **Note:** 
>
> RAM Availability
>
> The actual amount of RAM available to each cluster tier might be
> slightly less than the stated amount, due to memory that the kernel
> reserves.

The following cluster tiers are available:

| Cluster Tiers | Storage Range   | Default Storage | Default RAM |
| ------------- | --------------- | --------------- | ----------- |
| M0            | .5 GB           | .5 GB           | Shared      |
| Flex          | 5 GB            | 5 GB            | Flex        |
| M10           | 10 GB to 128 GB | 10 GB           | 1.7 GB      |
| M20           | 10 GB to 256 GB | 20 GB           | 3.8 GB      |
| M30           | 10 GB to 512 GB | 40 GB           | 8 GB        |
| M40           | 10 GB to 1 TB   | 80 GB           | 16 GB       |
| M50           | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 160 GB          | 32 GB       |
| M60           | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 320 GB          | 64 GB       |
| M80           | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 750 GB          | 128 GB      |
| M140          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 1000 GB         | 192 GB      |
| M200          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 1500 GB         | 256 GB      |
| M250          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 1750 GB         | 320 GB      |
| M300          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 2000 GB         | 360 GB      |
| M400          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 3000 GB         | 512 GB      |
| M600          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 4096 GB         | 640 GB      |

 Can use this tier for a multi-cloud cluster.

 Unavailable in the following regions:

- `AUSTRALIA_SOUTHEAST_1`

- `EUROPE_WEST_3`

- `NORTHEASTERN_ASIA_PACIFIC`

- `SOUTH_AMERICA_EAST_1`

- `EUROPE_WEST_8`

- `EUROPE_WEST_9`

- `EUROPE_SOUTHWEST_1`

 Atlas limits **R**-class instances
to the following regions:

- `CENTRAL_US`

- `EASTERN_US`

- `US_EAST_4`

- `WESTERN_US`

- `US_WEST_3`

- `US_WEST_4`

- `NORTH_AMERICA_NORTHEAST_1`

- `NORTH_AMERICA_NORTHEAST_2`

- `SOUTH_AMERICA_EAST_1`

* `ASIA_EAST_2`

* `ASIA_NORTHEAST_2`

* `ASIA_NORTHEAST_3`

* `ASIA_SOUTH_1`

* `ASIA_SOUTHEAST_2`

* `EASTERN_ASIA_PACIFIC`

* `NORTHEASTERN_ASIA_PACIFIC`

* `SOUTHEASTERN_ASIA_PACIFIC`

- `WESTERN_EUROPE`

- `EUROPE_NORTH_1`

- `EUROPE_WEST_2`

- `EUROPE_WEST_3`

- `EUROPE_WEST_4`

- `EUROPE_WEST_6`

- `EUROPE_WEST_8`

- `EUROPE_WEST_9`

- `EUROPE_SOUTHWEST_1`

> **Note:** 
>
> Cluster Tier & API Naming Conventions
>
> For purposes of management with the [Atlas Administration API](atlas-api), cluster tier names that are prepended with `R`
> instead of an `M` (`R40` for example) run a [class](storage-class-ui) version of the cluster. When
> [creating](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupcluster) or
> [modifying](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster) a
> cluster with the API, be sure to specify your desired cluster class
> by name with the
> `replicationSpecs[n].regionConfigs[m].electableSpecs.instanceSize`
> attribute.

> **Important:** 
>
> Multi-Cloud Low-CPU clusters
>
> Low-CPU cluster tiers (M40 Low-CPU, M50 Low-CPU, M60 Low-CPU, and so
> on) are available in [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region) as long as the cluster tier is
> available for all the regions that the cluster uses.

Workloads typically require less than `2TB` of storage.

Atlas configures the following resources automatically and does not
allow user modification:

- Storage Speed

- Encrypted Storage Volumes

sections.heading: GCP Zones
sections.body:
Each Google Cloud region includes a set number of independent zones. Each zone
has power, cooling, networking, and control planes that are isolated
from other zones.

For regions that have multiple zones, such as 2Z (for two zones) or 3Z
(for 3 zones), Atlas deploys clusters across these zones.

The Atlas Add New Cluster
form marks regions that support 3Z (Three Zone) clusters as
**Recommended**, as they provide higher availability.

To learn more, see [regions and zones](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/regions-zones)
in the Google documentation.

The number of zones in a region has no effect on the number of MongoDB
nodes Atlas can deploy. MongoDB Atlas clusters are always made of
replica sets with a minimum of three MongoDB nodes.

sections.heading: Regions with at Least Three Zones
sections.body:
If the selected Google Cloud region has at least three zones, Atlas
clusters are split across three zones. For example, a three node
replica set cluster would have one node deployed onto each zone.

![A 3-node replica set deployed across a 3-zone GCP region.](/reference/images/google-gcp-3az.svg)

3Z (Three Zone) clusters have higher availability
compared to 2Z (Two Zone) clusters. However, not all
regions support 3Z (Three Zone) clusters.

[^google-disk-docs]: To learn more, see [Storage Options](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/disks/) in the
    Google documentation.

sections.heading: Supported Regions
sections.body:
Atlas supports deploying separate [Search Nodes](configure-search-nodes) for `M10` and higher
clusters in most Google Cloud regions.

> **Note:** Search Nodes are currently not available in the following Google Cloud regions:
>
> - Johannesburg, South Africa (`africa-south1`)
>
> - Berlin, Germany (`europe-west10`)
>
> - Dammam, Saudi Arabia (`me-central2`)
>
> - Querétaro, Mexico (`northamerica-south1`)
>
> - South Carolina, USA (`us-east1`)

sections.heading: Available Search Tiers
sections.body:
Atlas provides different [Search Tier](select-tiers-for-search-nodes) for Search Nodes
deployed on Google Cloud.The following table lists the search tiers available for each class of
hardware (low-CPU and high-CPU), with default RAM and storage measured
in gibibytes (GiB):**Low-cpu:**Low-CPU| Instance Size | Instance Details                                      |
| ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| S30           | Default RAM: 8 GiBDefault Storage: 128 GiBvCPU: 2     |
| S40           | Default RAM: 16 GiBDefault Storage: 337 GiBvCPU: 2    |
| S50           | Default RAM: 32 GiBDefault Storage: 337 GiBvCPU: 4    |
| S60           | Default RAM: 64 GiBDefault Storage: 337 GiBvCPU: 8    |
| S70           | Default RAM: 96 GiBDefault Storage: 674 GiBvCPU: 12   |
| S80           | Default RAM: 128 GiBDefault Storage: 674 GiBvCPU: 16  |
| S90           | Default RAM: 256 GiBDefault Storage: 1348 GiBvCPU: 32 |**High-cpu:**High-CPU| Instance Size | Instance Details                                    |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| S20           | Default RAM: 4 GBDefault Storage: 126 GiBvCPU: 2    |
| S30           | Default RAM: 8 GBDefault Storage: 337 GiBvCPU: 4    |
| S40           | Default RAM: 16 GBDefault Storage: 337 GiBvCPU: 8   |
| S50           | Default RAM: 32 GBDefault Storage: 674 GiBvCPU: 16  |
| S60           | Default RAM: 64 GBDefault Storage: 1348 GiBvCPU: 32 |> **Note:** Most Search Nodes use NVMe (non-volatile memory express)-attached [Local SSD disks](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/disks/local-ssd) for storage. Search nodes
> with the following search tiers use
> SCSI (Small Computer System Interface)-attached SSD (Solid State Disk) [Persistent Disks](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/disks/persistent-disks) [^google-disk-docs]:
>
> - `S20` High-CPU Instances
>
> - `S30` Low-CPU Instances

sections.heading: Stream Processing Workspaces
sections.body:
> **Important:** To request an unlisted region, add your feedback
> to the [MongoDB Feedback Portal](https://feedback.mongodb.com/?category=7529546585152608284). Your
> input helps prioritize future expansions of Atlas Stream Processing coverage.

Atlas supports deploying [Stream Processing Workspaces](atlas-sp-manage-spi) in the following Google Cloud regions:

| Geography | GCP Region   | Location  | Atlas Region    |
| --------- | ------------ | --------- | --------------- |
| Americas  | us-central1  | Iowa, USA | CENTRAL\_US     |
| Europe    | europe-west1 | Belgium   | WESTERN\_EUROPE |

sections.heading: Integrations
sections.body:
Along with [Google Cloud Platform (GCP)](google-gcp), the following
product integrations enable applications running on Google Cloud, such as
Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud Functions, Google Cloud Run, and
Google App Engine, to use Atlas instances easily and securely.

sections.heading: Security and Identity Services
sections.body:
- Google Identity: Sign up and log into Atlas with Google

- Google Cloud Key Management Service (KMS):

  - [Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management](security-kms-encryption)

  - [Configure client-side field level encryption with KMS](manual/core/security-client-side-encryption-key-management)

sections.heading: Procurement
sections.body: - GCP Marketplace: [Pay for Atlas usage via GCP](https://console.cloud.google.com/marketplace/product/mongodb/atlas-pro?pli=1)

sections.heading: More Information
sections.body:
For more information on how to use Google Cloud with Atlas most effectively,
review the following best practices, guides, and case studies:

- Case Study: [Why build apps on a cloud-native database like MongoDB Atlas?](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/databases/why-build-apps-on-cloud-native-database-like-mongodb-atlas)

- Google Datastream: [Streamline your real-time data pipeline with Datastream and MongoDB](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/databases/get-real-time-analytics-data-with-datastream-and-mongodb)
